name: d3js-visualization
description: Professional data visualization creation using D3.js with support for interactive charts, custom visualizations, animations, and responsive design. Use for: (1) Creating custom interactive charts, (2) Building dashboards, (3) Network/graph visualizations, (4) Geographic data mapping, (5) Time series analysis, (6) Real-time data visualization, (7) Complex multi-dimensional data displays
capabilities: Create custom interactive charts including line, bar, scatter, pie, and area charts
Build force-directed network graphs and hierarchical visualizations
Implement smooth animations and transitions for data updates
Add tooltips, hover effects, and keyboard navigation for accessibility
Create responsive charts that adapt to different screen sizes
Parse and transform data from CSV, JSON, and other formats
author: AutumnsGrove
securitySummary: All 399 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The static analyzer confused markdown code formatting with shell execution, CSS selectors with reconnaissance, and D3 data binding property names with cryptographic keys. This is a legitimate D3.js visualization skill containing only standard charting code.
valueStatement: Build professional, interactive data visualizations with D3.js. Create custom charts, dashboards, network graphs, and real-time displays with full control over every visual element.
limitations: Does not generate 3D visualizations or WebGL-based graphics
Requires basic understanding of SVG coordinate system and D3 patterns
Performance decreases with more than 1,000 DOM elements (Canvas recommended for larger datasets)
Does not include pre-built chart components or templates beyond the provided examples

useCases.title: Build Dashboard Charts
useCases.description: Create interactive dashboards with multiple synchronized charts for business intelligence and reporting.

useCases.title: Custom Data Visuals
useCases.description: Implement unique data visualizations beyond what standard charting libraries can provide.

useCases.title: Explore Network Data
useCases.description: Visualize complex relationships, social networks, and hierarchical structures with force-directed graphs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Line Chart
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a simple time series chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a D3.js line chart showing data over time with x and y axes, labels, and a smooth line connecting data points.

promptTemplates.title: Bar Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a grouped bar chart in D3.js that compares multiple categories across different time periods with a legend.

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Tooltips
promptTemplates.prompt: Add tooltips to a D3.js chart that show detailed information when hovering over data points, with smooth positioning.

promptTemplates.title: Network Graph
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a force-directed network graph in D3.js with draggable nodes, link visualization, and node clustering by group.

bestPractices: Use the margin convention for proper spacing around chart elements
Apply key functions in data joins for consistent element-to-data matching
Use SVG for fewer than 1,000 elements, Canvas for larger datasets
Include proper axis labels and legends for accessibility
antiPatterns: Avoid hardcoding chart dimensions without responsive handling
Do not skip the enter/update/exit pattern in data joins
Avoid using the same color for adjacent data elements without clear distinction
Do not forget to clean up previous renders before updating charts

faq.question: What is D3.js?
faq.answer: D3.js is a JavaScript library for creating dynamic, interactive data visualizations in web browsers using SVG, HTML, and CSS.

faq.question: How is D3.js different from Chart.js?
faq.answer: D3.js provides complete control over every visual element but has a steeper learning curve, while Chart.js offers simpler standard charts with less customization.

faq.question: Can I use D3.js with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes, D3.js works well with React, Vue, Angular, and Svelte using integration patterns like controlled components or D3 for calculations only.

faq.question: How do I make charts responsive?
faq.answer: Use viewBox attributes, CSS max-width, and resize event listeners with debouncing to recalculate dimensions on window changes.

faq.question: What chart type should I use for time series?
faq.answer: Line charts are ideal for time series data. Use area charts when showing cumulative totals and bar charts for discrete time periods.

faq.question: How do I improve performance with large datasets?
faq.answer: Switch from SVG to Canvas rendering, aggregate or sample data, debounce resize handlers, and minimize unnecessary redraws.
